rationale.R1.1: Any meaningful opinion on this artifact requires reconstructing the contribution claim: multi-agent span-level arbitration closes the LLM-vs-NMT gap. Understanding what is actually being claimed is prerequisite to assessing it.
rationale.R1.2: The paper's core logical chain — specialization → debate → arbitration → quality gain — needs consistency checking. The ablation narrative (70%/40%/25% attribution) also requires verifying that the components sum coherently and the causal story holds.
rationale.R1.3: Three language-pair BLEU/COMET deltas and two ablation series are cited as evidence. Whether those numbers actually support the stated conclusions (arbitration as load-bearing, model-agnosticism, generalization without retraining) is central to any opinion.
rationale.R1.4: The 'unlike monolithic encoder-decoder' framing implies a novelty claim vs. prior multi-agent MT and ensemble/arbitration work. Assessing whether the delta over nearest prior art is real and correctly characterized is central if the opinion is scientific.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for a scientific/technical critique (evaluating whether the claims, evidence, and novelty hold up) rather than writing and presentation feedback (sentence quality, clarity, structure)?
